news_catalyst.headline: Post-SC Automatic Rally (AR); no new positive news; Open USD consortium (Visa/Mastercard/Stripe/BlackRock) threat unchanged
news_catalyst.summary: Circle's +4.31% is a post-SC Automatic Rally bounce from the July 1 SC lows at $61.80. The June 30 SC (2.86x volume on the Open USD announcement) was a genuine capitulation; the AR is expected Wyckoff behavior after a SC. However, the intraday rejection from $69.25 back to $64.62 close on 1.27x volume is a warning: sellers returned on the bounce. The Open USD consortium — Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, BlackRock, Coinbase, Ripple + 140 fintechs launching a rival USDC-killer — is a structural, not temporary, competitive threat.

technical.summary: The upper wick (close $4.63 below the intraday high) on 1.27x volume is a bearish signal within the post-SC bounce. A successful AR would close near the high on tapering volume. The $61.80 SC low remains critical support. The $69.25 intraday resistance is the AR ceiling to watch. The VCP structure (two contractions: 38.3%→19.2%) was broken by the June 30 SC; whether it reasserts depends on whether the Open USD threat is priced in at $61.80 or requires a deeper flush.

wyckoff_narrative: CRCL is at an ambiguous Wyckoff juncture. The June 30 SC (2.86x) was a genuine capitulation. The AR today (+4.31%) is normal post-SC behavior. The upper wick rejection (69.25→64.62) is the ambiguous signal: it could be a normal AR giveback, or it could signal that the SC did not clear all supply and another test of $61.80 is coming. The Open USD fundamental change makes this Wyckoff read harder than usual.

valuation.summary: DCF not feasible. Pre-announcement targets of 200% upside assumed USDC dominance; the Open USD consortium changes the TAM calculation. Avoid relying on pre-announcement analyst estimates.

risks: Upper wick today ($69.25 rejected to $64.62) shows sellers above $67 — AR may be stalling
$61.80 SC low must hold or distribution continues to $50
Open USD consortium is structurally permanent — business risk not headline risk
USDC market cap already declining from $80B YTD high before today's announcement
